records.ety: [OE. fre, freo, AS. fre, frī; akin to D. vrij, OS. & OHG. frī, G. frei, Icel. frī, Sw. & Dan. fri, Goth. freis, and also to Skr. prija beloved, dear, fr. prī to love, Goth. frijōn. Cf. Affray, Belfry, Friday, Friend, Frith inclosure.]

defs: Exempt from subjection to the will of others; not under restraint, control, or compulsion; able to follow one's own impulses, desires, or inclinations; determining one's own course of action; not dependent; at liberty.
quotes: That which has the power, or not the power, to operate, is that alone which is or is not free.

defs: Not under an arbitrary or despotic government; subject only to fixed laws regularly and fairly administered, and defended by them from encroachments upon natural or acquired rights; enjoying political liberty.
Liberated, by arriving at a certain age, from the control of parents, guardian, or master.
Not confined or imprisoned; released from arrest; liberated; at liberty to go.
Not subjected to the laws of physical necessity; capable of voluntary activity; endowed with moral liberty; -- said of the will.
quotes: Not free, what proof could they have given sincere Of true allegiance, constant faith, or love.

defs: Clear of offense or crime; guiltless; innocent.
quotes: My hands are guilty, but my heart is free.

defs: Unconstrained by timidity or distrust; unreserved; ingenuous; frank; familiar; communicative.
quotes: He was free only with a few.

defs: Unrestrained; immoderate; lavish; licentious; -- used in a bad sense.
quotes: The critics have been very free in their censures.
A man may live a free life as to wine or women.

defs: Not close or parsimonious; liberal; open-handed; lavish; as, free with his money.
Exempt; clear; released; liberated; not encumbered or troubled with; as, free from pain; free from a burden; -- followed by from, or, rarely, by of.
quotes: Princes declaring themselves free from the obligations of their treaties.

defs: Characteristic of one acting without restraint; charming; easy.
Ready; eager; acting without spurring or whipping; spirited; as, a free horse.
Invested with a particular freedom or franchise; enjoying certain immunities or privileges; admitted to special rights; -- followed by of.
quotes: He therefore makes all birds, of every sect, Free of his farm.

defs: Thrown open, or made accessible, to all; to be enjoyed without limitations; unrestricted; not obstructed, engrossed, or appropriated; open; -- said of a thing to be possessed or enjoyed; as, a free school.
quotes: Why, sir, I pray, are not the streets as free For me as for you?

defs: Not gained by importunity or purchase; gratuitous; spontaneous; as, free admission; a free gift.
Not arbitrary or despotic; assuring liberty; defending individual rights against encroachment by any person or class; instituted by a free people; -- said of a government, institutions, etc.
Certain or honorable; the opposite of base; as, free service; free socage.
Privileged or individual; the opposite of common; as, a free fishery; a free warren.
Not united or combined with anything else; separated; dissevered; unattached; at liberty to escape; as, free carbonic acid gas; free cells.
Freely; willingly.
quotes: I as free forgive you As I would be forgiven.

defs: Without charge; as, children admitted free.

records.ety: [OE. freen, freoien, AS. fregan. See Free, a.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Freed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Freeing.]

defs: To make free; to set at liberty; to rid of that which confines, limits, embarrasses, oppresses, etc.; to release; to disengage; to clear; -- followed by from, and sometimes by off; as, to free a captive or a slave; to be freed of these inconveniences.
quotes: Our land is from the rage of tigers freed.
Arise, . . . free thy people from their yoke.

defs: To remove, as something that confines or bars; to relieve from the constraint of.
quotes: This master key Frees every lock, and leads us to his person.

defs: To frank.
